subject: 5 things Democrats can deliver in 2026
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Democratic Party of Virginia, www
body:
With a Democratic trifecta, real change is finally on the table.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120555025/586632709/-793144256?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NDIzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4YTA2Mjk3Mi05MmQwLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=DMQ4phLXOZOMF1RfaiHmnP7YUtxaT-H3JuI8gUJnDdI=&emci=226f224b-a9cf-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=8a062972-92d0-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=31008101)
---
Hi Peter, With a new Democratic trifecta and expanded majorities, Virginia is positioned to make major progress in 2026.

**Here are five things Democrats can deliver going into next year’s session: **

**1\. Protect and expand reproductive freedom**
For two years, we stopped Republican attempts to ban abortion at 15 weeks or earlier. Now we can codify abortion rights in state law, protect access to contraception, and expand reproductive health care resources. Virginia can remain the last state in the South with real reproductive freedom, and we can make those protections stronger. **2\. Invest in Virginia’s public schools**
Virginia ranks 48th nationally in K–12 funding effort (JLARC 2023). That changes now. We can modernize the school funding formula, raise teacher pay above the national average, and address staff shortages while rebuilding crumbling infrastructure. **3\. Lower costs for working families**
Democrats can now advance economic relief that Republicans blocked, such as cracking down on predatory lenders, expanding childcare assistance, building more affordable housing, and speeding up clean energy expansion to reduce utility bills. **4\. Protect voting rights**
For two years, Republicans blocked bills to expand early voting, protect election workers, modernize voter registration, and combat disinformation. We can now push these reforms forward and make Virginia a national model for voting access. **5\. Build Virginia's clean-energy future**
Republicans tried repeatedly to gut the Virginia Clean Economy Act. Now we can strengthen clean-energy targets, bring manufacturing jobs to Virginia, and invest in coastal resiliency and rural energy development. This is what your support and commitment made possible. 2026 will be a year of action. [**Let’s get to work. Your donation today will help us capitalize on the momentum we started in November and continue to build a better Commonwealth together.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120555026/586632710/-2072720460?&refcode=E16504-20251203-FiveThings-SL-2026Race&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NDIzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4YTA2Mjk3Mi05MmQwLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=DMQ4phLXOZOMF1RfaiHmnP7YUtxaT-H3JuI8gUJnDdI=) _If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | | [DONATE $5 →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120555027/586632711/-2072720460?express_lane=true&&refcode=E16504-20251203-FiveThings-EG-2026Race&amount=5&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NDIzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4YTA2Mjk3Mi05MmQwLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=DMQ4phLXOZOMF1RfaiHmnP7YUtxaT-H3JuI8gUJnDdI=)

clean_body: With a Democratic trifecta, real change is finally on the table. | Hi Peter, With a new Democratic trifecta and expanded majorities, Virginia is positioned to make major progress in 2026. Here are five things Democrats can deliver going into next year’s session: 1\. Protect and expand reproductive freedom For two years, we stopped Republican attempts to ban abortion at 15 weeks or earlier. Now we can codify abortion rights in state law, protect access to contraception, and expand reproductive health care resources. Virginia can remain the last state in the South with real reproductive freedom, and we can make those protections stronger. 2\. Invest in Virginia’s public schools Virginia ranks 48th nationally in K–12 funding effort (JLARC 2023). That changes now. We can modernize the school funding formula, raise teacher pay above the national average, and address staff shortages while rebuilding crumbling infrastructure. 3\. Lower costs for working families Democrats can now advance economic relief that Republicans blocked, such as cracking down on predatory lenders, expanding childcare assistance, building more affordable housing, and speeding up clean energy expansion to reduce utility bills. 4\. Protect voting rights For two years, Republicans blocked bills to expand early voting, protect election workers, modernize voter registration, and combat disinformation. We can now push these reforms forward and make Virginia a national model for voting access. 5\. Build Virginia's clean-energy future Republicans tried repeatedly to gut the Virginia Clean Economy Act. Now we can strengthen clean-energy targets, bring manufacturing jobs to Virginia, and invest in coastal resiliency and rural energy development. This is what your support and commitment made possible. 2026 will be a year of action. = If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | = | = | | = | = — Team DPVA [content goes here] Forwarded this email? name: Beto O'Rourke
subject: This is the work that works
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY POWERED BY PEOPLE
body:
[1]Powered By People

I recently joined our Powered by People volunteers in Austin as they moved
us a little closer to victory in 2026.

We met up at Austin Community College at 8:30am, the coffee just kicking
in, the smiles still a little weak and cold from the chilly, rainy,
overcast morning.

But as we warmed up, and started smiling for real, and began introducing
ourselves to the people rushing by, we began to connect with the Texans
who will decide the outcome of the most important elections of our lives.

Nursing students on their way to their exams... animation students working
on their end-of-year projects... transfer students just back from certifying
their credits... a veteran from El Paso who moved to Austin last year for a
new start in life... we asked them each if we could check their voter
registration for them, get them registered if they weren’t already, answer
questions about elections and politics and then—critically—add them to our
network of voters who we will personally stay in touch with over the
course of the 2026 election cycle.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251203.P6YgsB&refcode=ema20251203-240501&t=1002&akid=240501%2E444211%2E_5fGt0 ]Powered by People volunteer registering a voter.

We ended up registering or updating registrations for 21 people and adding
a total of 74 new, young voters to our network.

We then drove to the UT campus and, with the partnership of the amazing
University Democrats, set up on Speedway where we did the same
thing—introduce ourselves to students and offer them the opportunity to
change the world by choosing to vote in our country’s elections.

For nearly five hours on this unusually cold, wet winter day, our
volunteers did the tough work that we know makes it more likely that
Democrats can win in Texas. I don’t know how effective TV ads are, or
social media campaigns, or whether people even look at the slick mailers
they get from candidates. But I do know that the young voters who we met
and registered and stayed in touch with in 2024 turned out at a rate of
79.5% (versus 42% nationally and 37% in Texas).

And I know that at UT, we registered or updated registrations for 218
people and added a total of 417 new, young voters to our network.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251203.P6YgsB&refcode=ema20251203-240501&t=1003&akid=240501%2E444211%2E_5fGt0 ]Powered by People volunteer registering a voter.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251203.P6YgsB&refcode=ema20251203-240501&t=1004&akid=240501%2E444211%2E_5fGt0 ]Beto with students

This is the work that works. And it’s not just ACC and UT, it’s happening
on college campuses across the state. And it’s not just what we did on
Monday, it’s what we’re doing every day until we win in November 2026.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251203.P6YgsB&refcode=ema20251203-240501&t=1005&akid=240501%2E444211%2E_5fGt0 ]I hope you will contribute to our ability to continue this work, and
more importantly, to expand our efforts to be on more college campuses,
find more young voters who we can bring into this election, do more of the
work that makes it more likely we win in Texas so that we can save this
country.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251203.P6YgsB&refcode=ema20251203-240501&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1024&akid=240501%2E444211%2E_5fGt0 ]DONATE $3

Thank you,

Beto

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to act. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://act.betoorourke.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1025&akid=240501%2E444211%2E_5fGt0

PAID FOR BY POWERED BY PEOPLE

| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---
Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.
---

Peter, This Giving Tuesday, the NDRC set an ambitious goal to raise $50,000 to safeguard our democracy from Republicans attempting to manipulate maps and cheat their way into power. **Right now, extremists are launching an increasing number of attacks on fair representation — and they can’t go unanswered. That’s why we’re EXTENDING our Giving Tuesday deadline until midnight tonight.** If this year has taught us anything, it’s that we’re at a critical inflection point for fairness in our democracy. We need all hands on deck to fight back against Republican efforts to rig the midterms and unfairly hold on to power. **This is your last chance to help reach our $50,000 goal:[Rush your donation right away to ensure the NDRC is fully prepared to fight for fair maps in the months and years ahead. ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120556131/586636131/-1027206931?refcode=don_ndrc_tier1_email_GivingTuesdayExtension_email_20251203-NDRC-Tier1-GivingTuesdayExtension_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20251203-NDRC-Tier1-GivingTuesdayExtension_&utm_campaign=GivingTuesdayExtension&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjIzNzczYjI3LTk0ZDAtZjAxMS04MTk1LTAwMGQzYTFkNThhYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=ksa_xM4hcYK66_1ZStYleY0fM4fq2P3zbQKmiCLLpuM=)** \---------- Forwarded Message ---------- | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120556132/586636132/-1027206931?refcode=don_ndrc_tier1_email_GivingTuesdayExtension_email_20251203-NDRC-Tier1-GivingTuesdayExtension_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20251203-NDRC-Tier1-GivingTuesdayExtension_&utm_campaign=GivingTuesdayExtension&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjIzNzczYjI3LTk0ZDAtZjAxMS04MTk1LTAwMGQzYTFkNThhYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=ksa_xM4hcYK66_1ZStYleY0fM4fq2P3zbQKmiCLLpuM=)

Peter,
Today is an important day for fundraising at the NDRC, so let me be direct about [**why your Giving Tuesday donation of any amount is so important.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120556133/586636133/-1027206931?refcode=don_ndrc_tier1_email_GivingTuesdayExtension_email_20251203-NDRC-Tier1-GivingTuesdayExtension_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20251203-NDRC-Tier1-GivingTuesdayExtension_&utm_campaign=GivingTuesdayExtension&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjIzNzczYjI3LTk0ZDAtZjAxMS04MTk1LTAwMGQzYTFkNThhYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=ksa_xM4hcYK66_1ZStYleY0fM4fq2P3zbQKmiCLLpuM=)
Across the country, fair representation is being threatened. Anti-democracy extremists have passed gerrymanders in Texas and North Carolina that strip minority communities of fair representation. Map manipulators are meddling in Florida, Indiana, and Kansas to try and rig the midterms. A Supreme Court ruling on the constitutionality of the Voting Rights Act could come down any day now.
**The threats to our democracy are happening _right now_. We can’t take any of it lightly.**
With so much at stake, this national day of giving is a chance for our entire community to come together and keep our grassroots fundraising strong, ensuring we have the resources to carry on the fight for a truly representative democracy.
**We’ve set an ambitious goal to raise $50,000 this Giving Tuesday to ensure we remain ready to respond to threats in 2026 and beyond.[Will you donate any amount before midnight to help us continue pushing back against attacks on fair maps right now?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120556134/586636134/-1027206931?refcode=don_ndrc_tier1_email_GivingTuesdayExtension_email_20251203-NDRC-Tier1-GivingTuesdayExtension_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20251203-NDRC-Tier1-GivingTuesdayExtension_&utm_campaign=GivingTuesdayExtension&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjIzNzczYjI3LTk0ZDAtZjAxMS04MTk1LTAwMGQzYTFkNThhYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=ksa_xM4hcYK66_1ZStYleY0fM4fq2P3zbQKmiCLLpuM=)**

When Attorney General Eric Holder and President Barack Obama founded the NDRC almost a decade ago, it was because Republicans were quietly gerrymandering their way to power in Congress and state legislatures by disenfranchising voters. Put simply, they were cheating.
This past year, we’ve witnessed them cheating their way to power explode out in the open. Donald Trump and his White House are undertaking an unprecedented assault on fair representation, openly coordinating with Republicans nationwide to redraw maps mid-decade — a blatant attempt to unfairly hold on to power.
**The good news is, this is what the NDRC was built for.** Together, with grassroots supporters like you, we’ve been fighting back every step of the way. And we’ve had huge wins with voters passing Proposition 50 in California, establishing a Democratic trifecta in Virginia, defending an independent-minded state Supreme Court in Pennsylvania, and so much more.
**But as the attacks on fair maps get more egregious, every dollar matters in ensuring the NDRC has the resources to fight back. We already know that our opponents are planning to take their fight to states like Florida and Indiana in the next few weeks — and they are extremely well-funded.[This Giving Tuesday, make a donation to help us continue this critical work and grow this grassroots movement.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120556142/586636143/-1027206931?refcode=don_ndrc_tier1_email_GivingTuesdayExtension_email_20251203-NDRC-Tier1-GivingTuesdayExtension_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20251203-NDRC-Tier1-GivingTuesdayExtension_&utm_campaign=GivingTuesdayExtension&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjIzNzczYjI3LTk0ZDAtZjAxMS04MTk1LTAwMGQzYTFkNThhYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=ksa_xM4hcYK66_1ZStYleY0fM4fq2P3zbQKmiCLLpuM=)**

clean_body: Rush your donation before midnight | Peter, This Giving Tuesday, the NDRC set an ambitious goal to raise $50,000 to safeguard our democracy from Republicans attempting to manipulate maps and cheat their way into power. Right now, extremists are launching an increasing number of attacks on fair representation — and they can’t go unanswered. That’s why we’re EXTENDING our Giving Tuesday deadline until midnight tonight. If this year has taught us anything, it’s that we’re at a critical inflection point for fairness in our democracy. We need all hands on deck to fight back against Republican efforts to rig the midterms and unfairly hold on to power. This is your last chance to help reach our $50,000 goal:= \---------- Forwarded Message ---------- | = Peter, Today is an important day for fundraising at the NDRC, so let me be direct about = Across the country, fair representation is being threatened. Anti-democracy extremists have passed gerrymanders in Texas and North Carolina that strip minority communities of fair representation. Map manipulators are meddling in Florida, Indiana, and Kansas to try and rig the midterms. A Supreme Court ruling on the constitutionality of the Voting Rights Act could come down any day now. The threats to our democracy are happening right now. We can’t take any of it lightly. With so much at stake, this national day of giving is a chance for our entire community to come together and keep our grassroots fundraising strong, ensuring we have the resources to carry on the fight for a truly representative democracy. We’ve set an ambitious goal to raise $50,000 this Giving Tuesday to ensure we remain ready to respond to threats in 2026 and beyond.= If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = | = = | = = | = When Attorney General Eric Holder and President Barack Obama founded the NDRC almost a decade ago, it was because Republicans were quietly gerrymandering their way to power in Congress and state legislatures by disenfranchising voters. Put simply, they were cheating. This past year, we’ve witnessed them cheating their way to power explode out in the open. Donald Trump and his White House are undertaking an unprecedented assault on fair representation, openly coordinating with Republicans nationwide to redraw maps mid-decade — a blatant attempt to unfairly hold on to power. The good news is, this is what the NDRC was built for. Together, with grassroots supporters like you, we’ve been fighting back every step of the way. And we’ve had huge wins with voters passing Proposition 50 in California, establishing a Democratic trifecta in Virginia, defending an independent-minded state Supreme Court in Pennsylvania, and so much more. But as the attacks on fair maps get more egregious, every dollar matters in ensuring the NDRC has the resources to fight back. We already know that our opponents are planning to take their fight to states like Florida and Indiana in the next few weeks — and they are extremely well-funded.= Thank you so much, Brianna Shamsuddoha Mobilization Director = = | | Paid for by the National Democratic Redistricting Committee, John Bisognano, President. | | | | | | | ---|---|---|---|---|---|--- National Democratic Redistricting Committee 712 H St NE, #25 Washington, DC 20002 United States if you'd like to receive fewer emails from the NDRC. Text MAPS to 36787, STOP to end. Msg and Data rates may apply

2026 starts with what we build right now.

| |

Hi friend, We’re heading into the final end-of-quarter of 2025, and the first EOQ of this campaign. It’s our chance to show that Massachusetts is ready for the change Seth is fighting for: new leadership, a break from the status quo, and real urgency in a moment that can’t wait six more years. This deadline isn’t just a benchmark. It’s the test of whether a campaign focused on honesty, accountability, and getting things done has the grassroots power to challenge an old playbook that’s failed too many families in our state. # In This Season of Hope There Is Beauty All Around Us

**Dear** **Friend** :
****
It’s Jim Ball with[Climate Hope Together](https://vvjoamr.clicks.mlsend.com/tb/c/eyJ2Ijoie1wiYVwiOjE5MTgzMTYsXCJsXCI6MTcyODA1NDM3NDk4ODUzMDA3LFwiclwiOjE3MjgwNTQ1MTI4OTcyNDc3Nn0iLCJzIjoiMmUzYmYwNWFiZTU3NjNkMyJ9). Those of us concerned about climate change can learn something valuable from this season of hope as we travel through the darkness in anticipation of the Winter Solstice and the coming of the gift of greater light into the world. Even as climate consequences bring destruction, desperation, death, and despair, [together](https://vvjoamr.clicks.mlsend.com/tb/c/eyJ2Ijoie1wiYVwiOjE5MTgzMTYsXCJsXCI6MTcyODA1NDM3NTAzMDQ3MzE2LFwiclwiOjE3MjgwNTQ1MTI4OTcyNDc3Nn0iLCJzIjoiYWQxNjY1YmE2MGFjMTdjMyJ9) we are beating back the darkness with the light. And with this light the gift of a special kind of sight has been given to us; we can begin to see that beauty is all around us; beauty is everywhere.
* In joining together with others in climate action there is beauty.
* In every idea that moves us forward there is beauty.
* In our relation to what is greater than ourselves as we inhabit the climate action space there is beauty.
* In the love and passion that moves us to action there is beauty.

Overcoming climate change, creating a just and prosperous sustainability, enhancing wellbeing for everyone and everything — each effort towards their creation is beautiful. Stopping bad stuff, setting wrong right, making things better — beautiful. Creating the greatest and most long-lasting social change movement in the history of the world — beautiful. We must awaken to all these gifts of beauty. We must experience them in their fullness and let them move and inspire us. We must cherish and nourish and protect them. We must share them. We must never forsake or ignore them. Because living in a beautiful world is what we hope for, beauty creates hope, and all hope is beautiful. Beauty will help keep us moving forward as we pull our destiny into the present and make our future come faster. In climate action space and time, where in freedom we have chosen to offer precious seconds, minutes, days, and years of our finite existence on this Earth to create a better world, there is beauty within us and all around us and between us and before us. Our communion with beauty is part of the fulfillment of our humanity. You and me and all of us are made to be beauty-creators. Let’s do so together. If you have found these words helpful, I hope you’ll [subscribe](https://vvjoamr.clicks.mlsend.com/tb/c/eyJ2Ijoie1wiYVwiOjE5MTgzMTYsXCJsXCI6MTcyODA1NDM3NTEwMzg3MzU2LFwiclwiOjE3MjgwNTQ1MTI4OTcyNDc3Nn0iLCJzIjoiNzFjMTI2ZGFkY2MzOWFkZiJ9) for free to [Climate Hope Together](https://vvjoamr.clicks.mlsend.com/tb/c/eyJ2Ijoie1wiYVwiOjE5MTgzMTYsXCJsXCI6MTcyODA1NDM3NTE1NjMwMjQwLFwiclwiOjE3MjgwNTQ1MTI4OTcyNDc3Nn0iLCJzIjoiNzdmYWIzMjRhZjFlMWFmNCJ9). Beauty is one of our [10 Movement Values](https://vvjoamr.clicks.mlsend.com/tb/c/eyJ2Ijoie1wiYVwiOjE5MTgzMTYsXCJsXCI6MTcyODA1NDM3NTE5ODI0NTUyLFwiclwiOjE3MjgwNTQ1MTI4OTcyNDc3Nn0iLCJzIjoiNDgxMDZlZTEzYTBlMGVmZCJ9). Let’s keep this conversation going through your subscription. Have a wonderful holiday season. And thanks for all you are doing to bring light and beauty and justice into our world.

Jim Ball _
__Climate activist and thinker since ‘92. ”Together we make the impossible possible and the possible actual and the actual beautiful and our future come faster.”_

In climate action space and time, where in freedom we have chosen to offer precious seconds, minutes, days, and years of our finite existence on this Earth to create a better world, there is beauty within us and all around us and between us and before us. Our communion with beauty is part of the fulfillment of our humanity. You and me and all of us are made to be beauty-creators. Let’s do so together. If you have found these words helpful, I hope you’ll for free to . Beauty is one of our . Let’s keep this conversation going through your subscription. Have a wonderful holiday season. And thanks for all you are doing to bring light and beauty and justice into our world. Jim Ball Climate activist and thinker since ‘92. ”Together we make the impossible possible and the possible actual and the actual beautiful and our future come faster.” | | | | | Subscribe for free to Climate Hope Together_ and stay plugged into hope. | | | | | | | Subscribe for free and stay plugged in to climate hope. | | | | | | Climate Hope Together www.climatehopetogether.com | | You received this email because your values align with ours as we seek the common good. |

subject: Want to make a difference close to home?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Martin Heinrich for Senate
body:
Getting involved locally is how we shape the future. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120556833/586638668/-272948131?refcode=AU_MTH_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20251203_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS84OTA0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1NDdjZGYwYi05N2QwLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=91lF50lMA3dIrbOQuwjJ1GEGuRl38CkdXBdTae8sMa8=&emci=2fa9b7ba-88d0-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=547cdf0b-97d0-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=252045)

Team, We talk a lot about what it really means to “take your country back”. It’s not about slogans. It’s about showing up where it counts – in our neighborhoods, our schools, our local elections, and the conversations we have every day. The truth is simple:**if we want a stronger, fairer future, we build it together.** That starts close to home. Vote in your local elections. Volunteer a few hours when you can. Get to know the people who live down the street. These small steps add up, and they’re how communities like ours stay resilient. If you’re in Albuquerque, this matters even more right now. The runoff elections on December 7th are a chance to shape the direction of our city — and they’re often decided by just a handful of votes. Make a plan to participate and encourage a friend or neighbor to do the same. [_**We’ll keep doing our part to bring people into the process and support leaders who put New Mexicans first. name: Adam Schwarze
subject: Help me embarrass Bernie Sanders
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Schwarze for Senate
body:
I’ve seen socialism crush economies firsthand: families starving under state control, initiative erased by endless mandates, freedom traded for false security. |

---
Peter,

Last week, Bernie Sanders—the self-proclaimed democratic socialist—threw his full weight behind my opponent in the United States Senate contest in which we’re candidates.

In his endorsement of Lt. Gov. Peggy Flanagan, Sanders called her the fighter we need to “ _**stand up for working people against the billionaires and corporate interests.**_ ”

**Translation:** She’s his pick to push for socialized, government-run healthcare, $17 minimum wage, higher taxes on job creators, and overturning Citizens United to flood D.C. with more big-government cash.

That’s not leadership.

**That’s the socialism I fought against overseas.**

I’ve trained allied navies in the South China Sea.

_**I’m Adam Schwarze—Marine, SEAL, nuclear strategist, capitalist warrior. I’ve deterred real enemies to protect free enterprise. Now I’ll deter D.C. weakness and socialist overreach.**_

As a _**Navy SEAL Officer**_ , I deployed _**nine times**_ across 70+ countries—from three combat tours in Iraq as a Marine infantryman, to commanding a Crisis Alert Force in the Middle East, to humanitarian missions in Africa.

I’ve seen socialism crush economies firsthand: families starving under state control, initiative erased by endless mandates, freedom traded for false security.

That’s why I risked my life—to defend American capitalism, the engine that built the greatest nation on earth.

Now, I’m risking my career and professional reputation to run for the United States Senate in Minnesota. I’m the underdog, but Republicans have a very real shot at flipping this Senate seat for the first time in _**DECADES.**_

name: Kristen McDonald Rivet
subject: I can’t thank you enough, friend.
body:
|
#
Friend, **Thanks to you, we hit our November fundraising goal.** I’m deeply thankful for all the support you’ve given to this campaign – whether you chipped in, shared a post, or simply took time out of your day to read our message – it means a lot to me. That being said, we’ve still got work to do. Working families throughout our community are still forced to grapple with surging health care costs, essential programs being gutted, and an overall increase in the cost of living – all challenges I am working every single day to address in Congress. As we head into the last month of the year and our public end-of-quarter deadline, it’s more crucial than ever that we keep our foot on the gas. This seat remains a top target for Republicans, and in a race as close as ours, we can’t afford to give them even an inch. We’re working to create something real here, and with your support, I’m confident that we'll continue this momentum, and have a strong finish at the end of the year. Thank you again for standing with me. clean_body: | Friend, Thanks to you, we hit our November fundraising goal. I’m deeply thankful for all the support you’ve given to this campaign – whether you chipped in, shared a post, or simply took time out of your day to read our message – it means a lot to me. That being said, we’ve still got work to do. Working families throughout our community are still forced to grapple with surging health care costs, essential programs being gutted, and an overall increase in the cost of living – all challenges I am working every single day to address in Congress. As we head into the last month of the year and our public end-of-quarter deadline, it’s more crucial than ever that we keep our foot on the gas. This seat remains a top target for Republicans, and in a race as close as ours, we can’t afford to give them even an inch. We’re working to create something real here, and with your support, I’m confident that we'll continue this momentum, and have a strong finish at the end of the year. Thank you again for standing with me. More soon, Kristen | = | We'd hate to see you go, but you can click here if you'd like to . Thank you for supporting Kristen McDonald Rivet for Congress! Kristen for Michigan P.O. Box 854 Bay City, MI 48707 United States

# **Trump grants pardon to Democratic congressman who challenged Biden border**
President Donald Trump[ ](https://substack.com/redirect/2b5893cd-af02-4d7f-9e0a-a4bc909c6c32?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**[pardoned Texas Democratic Representative](https://substack.com/redirect/2b5893cd-af02-4d7f-9e0a-a4bc909c6c32?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**[ ](https://substack.com/redirect/2b5893cd-af02-4d7f-9e0a-a4bc909c6c32?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)Henry Cuellar and his wife Imelda on Wednesday, erasing federal corruption charges Trump claims were politically motivated punishment for the lawmaker's vocal opposition to Biden's immigration approach.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/2b5893cd-af02-4d7f-9e0a-a4bc909c6c32?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Political persecution alleged:** Trump contended the Biden administration deployed the FBI and Justice Department against Cuellar after the congressman broke ranks to challenge the president's border security record starting in early 2021, repeatedly demanding Biden visit the southern border and condemning what he termed an unfolding crisis.
* **Serious accusations dropped:** Federal authorities had accused the pair of pocketing $600,000 through fraudulent consulting arrangements with an Azerbaijani state energy firm and a Mexican financial institution spanning 2014 to 2021, alleging Cuellar leveraged his influential committee assignments to shape foreign policy decisions and lean on high-ranking officials.
* **Staying with Democrats despite Trump rescue:** Cuellar expressed gratitude for what he called Trump's leadership while simultaneously filing paperwork to run for Congress again under the Democratic banner, despite having supported impeachment efforts against Trump twice and now benefiting from presidential clemency that wipes away potential decades behind bars.

* * *
# **Rep. Jasmine Crockett faces $3,000 unpaid lien on Dallas luxury condo**
Texas Democratic Representative Jasmine Crockett **[owes over $3,000 in unpaid homeowners association fees](https://substack.com/redirect/41f13207-baa9-40b7-adeb-3f9e974d6b64?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** on her Dallas condominium, with records showing she "continues to fail and refuse" to settle the debt.
* **Default and property restrictions:** The Westside Condominium Association filed a $3,047.79 lien against Crockett's property in April 2024 for unpaid assessments and related charges, preventing her from selling the gated community residence she purchased in 2014 until the obligation is satisfied.
* **Senate ambitions amid scrutiny:** The 44-year-old lawmaker is actively considering a Senate run after recent polling suggested she could win, telling CNN she would decide by Thanksgiving with a December 8 filing deadline looming, even as she faces questions about spending approximately $75,000 in campaign funds this year on upscale hotels and travel.
* **Campaign spending patterns:** Federal Election Commission records reveal Crockett's political operation paid thousands to luxury properties including over $5,300 at the West Hollywood Edition in Los Angeles, nearly $1,200 at the Times Square Edition in New York, and roughly $6,000 combined at Martha's Vineyard accommodations, while also withdrawing $50,000 for security expenses she attributes to threats.

* * *
# Scott Bessent slams NYT for partisan reporting on Biden vs Trump health
Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent **[pushed back against liberal anchor Andrew Ross Sorkin](https://substack.com/redirect/9b22a913-d9d3-4add-b403-26b19068441a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** at the New York Times' DealBook Summit, accusing the outlet of covering up Joe Biden's "diminished capacity" while questioning Trump's health, noting that Biden's administration "did not have a cabinet meeting" for ten months and asking "How are you going to invoke the 25th Amendment if the cabinet secretaries never see the president?"
[](https://substack.com/redirect/657fb074-34a7-490c-9c61-643174e05f94?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* **White House releases MRI results showing Trump in "excellent health":** After speculation swirled following Trump's October MRI scan and his July diagnosis of chronic venous insufficiency (CVI), the White House released findings this week from the president's physician, US Navy Captain Sean Barbabella, stating the "advanced imaging" of Trump's heart and abdomen revealed no abnormalities and confirmed he is in "excellent health," with his cardiovascular system showing "no evidence of arterial narrowing."
* **Questions arise after NYT report on Trump 's schedule:** The scrutiny intensified after the New York Times ran a piece last week claiming Trump's schedule was "more stripped back compared to his first term" and that he "frequently appeared tired," prompting the 79-year-old president to furiously respond by calling an NYT journalist "ugly" and insisting he is in better health than ever, while Bessent noted Treasury colleagues said he sees Trump "more in a day than my predecessor saw Biden in half a year."

* * *
# **FBI leaders Patel and Bongino reject internal criticism**
FBI Director Kash Patel and Deputy Director Dan Bongino dismissed a scathing internal assessment from current and former bureau personnel, insisting their aggressive overhaul has produced unprecedented results in **[crime reduction](https://substack.com/redirect/f92c51a6-7e06-4ee2-817d-9b5d4dd96eb8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** and operational efficiency.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/f92c51a6-7e06-4ee2-817d-9b5d4dd96eb8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Blistering internal review:** A 115-page report crafted by an alliance of active-duty and retired FBI agents and analysts in the style of an intelligence product slammed Patel as "in over his head," while characterizing Bongino as "something of a clown," based on accounts from 24 sources describing conditions inside the restructured bureau.
* **Defenders tout transformation:** Bongino dismissed the criticism as "noise from the small circle of disgruntled former agents still loyal to the old Comey-Wray model," arguing the leadership team's responsibility lies with the American people rather than internal critics, while emphasizing improved accountability standards, significant cost savings, and a restored mission-focused culture.
* **Reform achievements highlighted:** Patel outlined structural changes including pushing talent from Washington headquarters into field operations, establishing new capabilities like a counter-drone training program, overhauling Freedom of Information Act responsiveness, and eliminating billions in wasteful spending, crediting these moves with producing historic crime decreases and record arrests across violent crime, espionage, terrorism, and child exploitation categories.

Senate ambitions amid scrutiny: The 44-year-old lawmaker is actively considering a Senate run after recent polling suggested she could win, telling CNN she would decide by Thanksgiving with a December 8 filing deadline looming, even as she faces questions about spending approximately $75,000 in campaign funds this year on upscale hotels and travel. Campaign spending patterns: Federal Election Commission records reveal Crockett's political operation paid thousands to luxury properties including over $5,300 at the West Hollywood Edition in Los Angeles, nearly $1,200 at the Times Square Edition in New York, and roughly $6,000 combined at Martha's Vineyard accommodations, while also withdrawing $50,000 for security expenses she attributes to threats. Scott Bessent slams NYT for partisan reporting on Biden vs Trump health Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent at the New York Times' DealBook Summit, accusing the outlet of covering up Joe Biden's "diminished capacity" while questioning Trump's health, noting that Biden's administration "did not have a cabinet meeting" for ten months and asking "How are you going to invoke the 25th Amendment if the cabinet secretaries never see the president?" White House releases MRI results showing Trump in "excellent health": After speculation swirled following Trump's October MRI scan and his July diagnosis of chronic venous insufficiency (CVI), the White House released findings this week from the president's physician, US Navy Captain Sean Barbabella, stating the "advanced imaging" of Trump's heart and abdomen revealed no abnormalities and confirmed he is in "excellent health," with his cardiovascular system showing "no evidence of arterial narrowing." Questions arise after NYT report on Trump 's schedule: The scrutiny intensified after the New York Times ran a piece last week claiming Trump's schedule was "more stripped back compared to his first term" and that he "frequently appeared tired," prompting the 79-year-old president to furiously respond by calling an NYT journalist "ugly" and insisting he is in better health than ever, while Bessent noted Treasury colleagues said he sees Trump "more in a day than my predecessor saw Biden in half a year." FBI leaders Patel and Bongino reject internal criticism FBI Director Kash Patel and Deputy Director Dan Bongino dismissed a scathing internal assessment from current and former bureau personnel, insisting their aggressive overhaul has produced unprecedented results in and operational efficiency. | | ---|---|--- Blistering internal review: A 115-page report crafted by an alliance of active-duty and retired FBI agents and analysts in the style of an intelligence product slammed Patel as "in over his head," while characterizing Bongino as "something of a clown," based on accounts from 24 sources describing conditions inside the restructured bureau. Defenders tout transformation: Bongino dismissed the criticism as "noise from the small circle of disgruntled former agents still loyal to the old Comey-Wray model," arguing the leadership team's responsibility lies with the American people rather than internal critics, while emphasizing improved accountability standards, significant cost savings, and a restored mission-focused culture. Reform achievements highlighted: Patel outlined structural changes including pushing talent from Washington headquarters into field operations, establishing new capabilities like a counter-drone training program, overhauling Freedom of Information Act responsiveness, and eliminating billions in wasteful spending, crediting these moves with producing historic crime decreases and record arrests across violent crime, espionage, terrorism, and child exploitation categories. * You're currently a free subscriber to . For the full experience, | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Politibrawl 548 Market Street PMB 72296, San Francisco, CA 94104 ? ?

name: Mark Kelly
subject: I will not give up on our country
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY HICKENLOOPER FOR COLORADO
body:
It has given me too much, and I’ll never be able to repay that debt. I still believe in our democracy, our values, and our Constitution.

Folks, If you can believe it, things didn’t stop when Trump called for my hanging. It didn’t stop with the threat of a court-martial from Pete Hegseth, either. Because now, the FBI wants to talk to me and other members of Congress. All for saying things the president didn’t like to hear. If you’re feeling like this isn’t the country you grew up in, I don’t blame you. But I hope you aren’t giving up on it, folks. I certainly haven’t. This country has given me too much, and I’ll never be able to repay that debt. I still believe in our democracy, our values, and our Constitution. There’s no chance in hell that somebody like Donald Trump is going to stop me from serving the people of this nation. None. If he thought all of this was going to shut me up, he’s got another thing coming. And if you’d be willing to help support me as we gear up to fight back against the attacks coming our way from Donald Trump and his Administration, please: [_**Split a donation between John Hickenlooper and me. It would mean a lot.**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120557700/586641339/-1485135638?amounts=5%2C10%2C15%2C25%2C50%2C100%2C250&amount=10&refcode=email_120325_kelly&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83MjY1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNjMxZWNiNi05OWQwLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=eCxudlCC2KYwKNWuCJ_lC-tu5bzc1saCBMpMJVD24bY=) _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately and be split between Mark Kelly and John Hickenlooper:_ | | | | [Donate $5 →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120557701/586641340/-1485135638?amounts=5%2C10%2C15%2C25%2C50%2C100%2C250&amount=5&express_lane=true&refcode=email_120325_kelly&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83MjY1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNjMxZWNiNi05OWQwLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=eCxudlCC2KYwKNWuCJ_lC-tu5bzc1saCBMpMJVD24bY=)

name: Mandela Barnes
subject: My Republican opponent just called me a dangerous extremist
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Mandela for Wisconsin
body:
Are you serious?
| Hi there, it’s Mandela Barnes. It’s only the second day of my campaign, and my Republican opponent is already trying to spin the truth about who I am. [If you want to help me fight back, you can use this link to chip in.](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/1212?c0=mb-em-fr-dec25.SSbw4W&amount=15&refcode=SBD_MB_EM_FR_120325_Opponent_Tier1_D1_S1&t=2&akid=5691%2E196590%2EnK-4oN)** My opponent called me a “dangerous far-left extremist.” But let me ask you, are these things extreme? | Is it extreme to advocate for every Wisconsinite to have access to affordable healthcare? **I don’t think so.** Is it extreme to dream of a Wisconsin where working families who play by the rules can make ends meet, and even afford to go on vacation once in a while? **I wouldn’t say it is.** And is it so extreme to close the same loopholes for billionaires that are bleeding our middle class dry? **Absolutely not.**
---
It is very telling that Republicans think these goals are dangerous.
But I’m running for governor to ensure that nobody in Wisconsin goes without food, shelter, or healthcare. Because the Wisconsin Way is to bring each other up, not pull them down. It is to actually deliver outcomes, not create chaos and division.
[If these sound like principles you can get behind, please chip in $27 or whatever you can to support my campaign.](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/1212?c0=mb-em-fr-dec25.SSbw4W&amount=27&refcode=SBD_MB_EM_FR_120325_Opponent_Tier1_D1_S1&t=3&akid=5691%2E196590%2EnK-4oN)**

name: Texas Organizing Project
subject: Staying warm and focused / Mantenerse cálido y concentrado 🔥
body:
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/TSM/TSMTO/1/45640/images/PNG%202023%20TOP%20Newsletter%20Header.png]
Lea el boletín en español abajo [#Español]
Joining flood survivors to demand the recovery fund Texans deserve
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/TSM/TSMTO/1/45640/images/2025%20flood%20survivors%20-%20Austin%20rally.JPG]
Natural disasters impact Texans of all walks of life and across all parts of our state. That's why TOP was proud to join survivors of this summer's tragic Central Texas floods for a press conference and rally outside the Governor's Mansion on Nov. 21, to urge Gov. Abbott to use his executive power for good to deliver meaningful disaster recovery relief for thousands of families by starting the Texas Forever Fund. Watch a news clip recap of the event. [https://www.kvue.com/article/news/local/sandy-creek-survivors-rally-for-disaster-fund-to-recover/269-cb02c594-ef32-48ed-b1fb-c0bea5c3b31e]
Climate change continues to add to the severity and unpredictability of the weather we face here in Texas. Whether it be floods, freezes, tornados, fires, or hurricanes, the truth is it's often working families who bear the brunt of these natural disasters. Our state government has the financial means to help impacted families get back on their feet after these catastrophic events; Greg Abbott has to show up and do what's right, though.
https://fundtexasforever.org/ [https://fundtexasforever.org/]
THIS MONTH:
Learn about TOP's latest Right2Justice efforts in Harris County
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/TSM/TSMTO/1/45640/images/Gavel-Scales-of-Justice.jpg]
We as community members have the collective power to shape policies impacting policing and our local justice systems. We can shift away from harmful measures that prioritized punishment, leading to mass incarceration, and instead adopt restorative practices that center our humanity and allow more people to thrive.
If you're a Harris County resident interested in learning more about this latest TOP Right2Justice effort and how you can take part, email Dr. Doshie Piper at dpiper@organizetexas.org [dpiper@organizetexas.org] for upcoming event details.
DON'T MISS IT:
Healthcare Marketplace open enrollment deadlines are approaching
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/TSM/TSMTO/1/45640/images/Stetho-Around-Texas_2.jpg]
While this year's open enrollment period has brought with it uncertainty and confusion for the more than 24 million people who buy health insurance through the federal and state Affordable Care Act marketplaces, there are still steps marketplace shoppers can take to ensure they make informed choices for the upcoming plan year.
TOP believes healthcare is a human right, and while some elected officials (we're looking at you, Congressional Republicans) choose to play politics with this, the need to share vital healthcare information with our fellow community members has never been gre ater.
In most states (including Texas), people must enroll by Dec. 15 for coverage starting Jan. 1, and by Jan. 15 for coverage starting Feb. 1.
https://health.usnews.com/wellness/articles/how-to-pick-a-health-insurance-plan [https://health.usnews.com/wellness/articles/how-to-pick-a-health-insurance-plan]
TOP Tip:
🎉 Pitch in as TOP celebrates its first 15 years 🎉
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/TSM/TSMTO/1/45640/images/SA%20members%20at%20press%20conf.%202025.jpg]
For the past 15 years, TOP has built people power and political power alongside Black and Latino Texans to win policies that aim to lift up ALL in our state — no matter our race, age, faith, gender, or income. And through relentless organizing and solidarity, we know the sky's the limit for the future we can win. Help us go for another impactful 15 years by contributing $15 to TOP today! [https://secure.everyaction.com/_ew4evGgs0i4Ml7-RjD4tQ2]
Español
Uniéndonos a los sobrevivientes de las inundaciones para exigir el fondo de recuperación que los tejanos merecen
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/TSM/TSMTO/1/45640/images/2025%20flood%20survivors%20-%20Austin%20rally.JPG]
Los desastres naturales afectan a tejanos de todos los ámbitos y en todas las regiones de nuestro estado. Por eso, TOP se enorgulleció de unirse a los sobrevivientes de las trágicas inundaciones en el centro de Texas este verano para una conferencia de prensa y un mitin frente a la Mansión del Gobernador, para exigirle al gobernador Abbott que use su poder ejecutivo para hacer el bien y brindar una recuperación significativa a miles de familias mediante la creación del Texas Forever Fund . Mira aquí un resumen del evento en una nota de noticias. [https://www.kvue.com/article/news/local/sandy-creek-survivors-rally-for-disaster-fund-to-recover/269-cb02c594-ef32-48ed-b1fb-c0bea5c3b31e]
El cambio climático continúa aumentando la gravedad e imprevisibilidad del clima que enfrentamos en Texas. Ya sean inundaciones, heladas, tornados, incendios o huracanes, la realidad es que son las familias trabajadoras quienes cargan con la peor parte de estos desastres naturales. Nuestro gobierno estatal tiene los recursos financieros para ayudar a las familias afectadas a recuperarse después de estos eventos catastróficos; Greg Abbott, sin embargo, tiene que presentarse y hacer lo correcto.
https://fundtexasforever.org/ [https://fundtexasforever.org/]
ESTE MES:
Infórmese sobre las últimas iniciativas Right2Justice de TOP en el condado de Harris
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/TSM/TSMTO/1/45640/images/Gavel-Scales-of-Justice.jpg]
Como miembros de la comunidad, tenemos el poder colectivo de definir políticas que impactan la actuación policial y nuestros sistemas de justicia locales. Podemos alejarnos de medidas perjudiciales que priorizan el castigo, lo que conduce al encarcelamiento masivo, y en su lugar adoptar prácticas restaurativas que prioricen nuestra humanidad y permitan que más personas prosperen.
Si reside en el Condado de Harris y está interesado en obtener más información sobre esta última iniciativa de TOP Right2Justice y cómo participar, envíe un correo electrónico a la Dra. Doshie Piper a dpiper@organizetexas.org [dpiper@organizetexas.org] para obtener información sobre el próximo evento.
NO TE LO PIERDAS:
Se acercan las fechas límite de inscripción abierta del Mercado de Seguros Médicos
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/TSM/TSMTO/1/45640/images/Stetho-Around-Texas_2.jpg]
Aunque el período de inscripción abierta de este año ha generado incertidumbre y confusión para los más de 24 millones de personas que contratan seguro médico a través de los mercados federales y estatales de la Ley del Cuidado de Salud a Bajo Precio, aún existen medidas que quienes compran en el mercado pueden tomar para asegurarse de tomar decisiones informadas para el próximo año de plan.
TOP cree que la atención médica es un derecho humano, y aunque algunos funcionarios electos (los estamos hablando a ustedes, congresistas republicanos) optan por jugar políticamente con esto, la necesidad de compartir información vital sobre la salud con los miembros de nuestra comunidad nunca ha sido mayor.
En la mayoría de los estados (incluido Texas), las personas deben inscribirse antes del 15 de diciembre para la cobertura a partir del 1 de enero, y antes del 15 de enero para la cobertura a partir del 1 de febrero.
https://health.usnews.com/wellness/articles/how-to-pick-a-health-insurance-plan [https://health.usnews.com/wellness/articles/how-to-pick-a-health-insurance-plan]
Consejos de TOP: 🎉 Únete a TOP en su 15o aniversario 🎉
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/TSM/TSMTO/1/45640/images/SA%20members%20at%20press%20conf.%202025.jpg]
Durante los últimos 15 años, TOP ha fortalecido el poder popular y político junto con la comunidad morena y latina de Texas para lograr políticas que buscan impulsar a TODOS en nuestro estado, sin importar raza, edad, religión, género o ingresos. Y mediante la organización incansable y la solidaridad, sabemos que el cielo es el límite para el futuro que podemos ganar. ¡Ayúdanos a lograr otros 15 años de impacto contribuyendo con $15 a TOP hoy mismo! clean_body: [] Lea el boletín en español abajo [#Español] Joining flood survivors to demand the recovery fund Texans deserve [] Natural disasters impact Texans of all walks of life and across all parts of our state. That's why TOP was proud to join survivors of this summer's tragic Central Texas floods for a press conference and rally outside the Governor's Mansion on Nov. 21, to urge Gov. Abbott to use his executive power for good to deliver meaningful disaster recovery relief for thousands of families by starting the Texas Forever Fund. Watch a news clip recap of the event. [] Climate change continues to add to the severity and unpredictability of the weather we face here in Texas. Whether it be floods, freezes, tornados, fires, or hurricanes, the truth is it's often working families who bear the brunt of these natural disasters. Our state government has the financial means to help impacted families get back on their feet after these catastrophic events; Greg Abbott has to show up and do what's right, though. [] THIS MONTH: Learn about TOP's latest Right2Justice efforts in Harris County [] We as community members have the collective power to shape policies impacting policing and our local justice systems. We can shift away from harmful measures that prioritized punishment, leading to mass incarceration, and instead adopt restorative practices that center our humanity and allow more people to thrive. If you're a Harris County resident interested in learning more about this latest TOP Right2Justice effort and how you can take part, email Dr. Doshie Piper at dpiper@organizetexas.org [dpiper@organizetexas.org] for upcoming event details. DON'T MISS IT: Healthcare Marketplace open enrollment deadlines are approaching [] While this year's open enrollment period has brought with it uncertainty and confusion for the more than 24 million people who buy health insurance through the federal and state Affordable Care Act marketplaces, there are still steps marketplace shoppers can take to ensure they make informed choices for the upcoming plan year. TOP believes healthcare is a human right, and while some elected officials (we're looking at you, Congressional Republicans) choose to play politics with this, the need to share vital healthcare information with our fellow community members has never been gre ater. In most states (including Texas), people must enroll by Dec. 15 for coverage starting Jan. 1, and by Jan. 15 for coverage starting Feb. 1. [] TOP Tip: 🎉 Pitch in as TOP celebrates its first 15 years 🎉 [] For the past 15 years, TOP has built people power and political power alongside Black and Latino Texans to win policies that aim to lift up ALL in our state — no matter our race, age, faith, gender, or income. And through relentless organizing and solidarity, we know the sky's the limit for the future we can win. Help us go for another impactful 15 years by contributing $15 to TOP today! [] Español Uniéndonos a los sobrevivientes de las inundaciones para exigir el fondo de recuperación que los tejanos merecen [] Los desastres naturales afectan a tejanos de todos los ámbitos y en todas las regiones de nuestro estado. Por eso, TOP se enorgulleció de unirse a los sobrevivientes de las trágicas inundaciones en el centro de Texas este verano para una conferencia de prensa y un mitin frente a la Mansión del Gobernador, para exigirle al gobernador Abbott que use su poder ejecutivo para hacer el bien y brindar una recuperación significativa a miles de familias mediante la creación del Texas Forever Fund . Mira aquí un resumen del evento en una nota de noticias. [] El cambio climático continúa aumentando la gravedad e imprevisibilidad del clima que enfrentamos en Texas. Ya sean inundaciones, heladas, tornados, incendios o huracanes, la realidad es que son las familias trabajadoras quienes cargan con la peor parte de estos desastres naturales. Nuestro gobierno estatal tiene los recursos financieros para ayudar a las familias afectadas a recuperarse después de estos eventos catastróficos; Greg Abbott, sin embargo, tiene que presentarse y hacer lo correcto. [] ESTE MES: Infórmese sobre las últimas iniciativas Right2Justice de TOP en el condado de Harris [] Como miembros de la comunidad, tenemos el poder colectivo de definir políticas que impactan la actuación policial y nuestros sistemas de justicia locales. Podemos alejarnos de medidas perjudiciales que priorizan el castigo, lo que conduce al encarcelamiento masivo, y en su lugar adoptar prácticas restaurativas que prioricen nuestra humanidad y permitan que más personas prosperen. Si reside en el Condado de Harris y está interesado en obtener más información sobre esta última iniciativa de TOP Right2Justice y cómo participar, envíe un correo electrónico a la Dra. Doshie Piper a dpiper@organizetexas.org [dpiper@organizetexas.org] para obtener información sobre el próximo evento. NO TE LO PIERDAS: Se acercan las fechas límite de inscripción abierta del Mercado de Seguros Médicos [] Aunque el período de inscripción abierta de este año ha generado incertidumbre y confusión para los más de 24 millones de personas que contratan seguro médico a través de los mercados federales y estatales de la Ley del Cuidado de Salud a Bajo Precio, aún existen medidas que quienes compran en el mercado pueden tomar para asegurarse de tomar decisiones informadas para el próximo año de plan. TOP cree que la atención médica es un derecho humano, y aunque algunos funcionarios electos (los estamos hablando a ustedes, congresistas republicanos) optan por jugar políticamente con esto, la necesidad de compartir información vital sobre la salud con los miembros de nuestra comunidad nunca ha sido mayor. En la mayoría de los estados (incluido Texas), las personas deben inscribirse antes del 15 de diciembre para la cobertura a partir del 1 de enero, y antes del 15 de enero para la cobertura a partir del 1 de febrero. [] Consejos de TOP: 🎉 Únete a TOP en su 15o aniversario 🎉 [] Durante los últimos 15 años, TOP ha fortalecido el poder popular y político junto con la comunidad morena y latina de Texas para lograr políticas que buscan impulsar a TODOS en nuestro estado, sin importar raza, edad, religión, género o ingresos. Y mediante la organización incansable y la solidaridad, sabemos que el cielo es el límite para el futuro que podemos ganar. ¡Ayúdanos a lograr otros 15 años de impacto contribuyendo con $15 a TOP hoy mismo! subject: 'Tis the Season For Signatures
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Vote Kern]( "Paid For By Vote Kern")
body:
It takes just 1 minute |
---
| | [ ](https://votekern.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=2add33eab54cf78e2fdebc3c8&id=84ebcf2fc7&e=b78dc6f299)
---
|
---
| **Anthony Kern For State Senate:

Sign my [e-qual](https://votekern.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=2add33eab54cf78e2fdebc3c8&id=c00517c76c&e=b78dc6f299) to put me on the ballot!**
---
|
---
| Supporters and Friends,

As we deck the halls and hoist up the tree, let’s remember that it’s not just holiday season: It’s also signature season! I’m collecting signatures so I qualify for ballot access—just a couple thousand will square me away so you and I can focus on improving life here in LD-27. [**It won’t take longer than two minutes of your time**](https://votekern.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=2add33eab54cf78e2fdebc3c8&id=a84faa6cc7&e=b78dc6f299). _Do you have your own ideas on how we can make our district the envy of Arizona?_ I’d love to hear them. _You’ll have the perfect opportunity by signing up to host a meet and greet at your own home_! Gather a small group of friends, family, coworkers, anyone like-minded who’s ready to return good government to Phoenix and I’ll be there to join you. We can discuss my program to assist _President Trump_ in defending our border, giving cover to law enforcement as they protect our community, and aiding families in the fight for better schools, absent any state indoctrination. Responsive government won’t just start **when I reenter the Arizona Senate**. It starts with engaging with constituents like you here and now. My campaign can travel right to your living room, but before that can happen [**I need you to follow this link and lend your signature to confirm my name on the ballot**](https://votekern.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=2add33eab54cf78e2fdebc3c8&id=0d221330f7&e=b78dc6f299). I can confirm that [E-QUAL](https://votekern.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=2add33eab54cf78e2fdebc3c8&id=c6235fe9d1&e=b78dc6f299) is the simplest and safest method for acquiring signatures and ensuring that our voting process is protected from fraud. Unlike in-person gathering, digital sign-ups require confirmed identification and cannot be forged. By using [E-QUAL](https://votekern.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=2add33eab54cf78e2fdebc3c8&id=143eb960d1&e=b78dc6f299), you’re helping to fortify our elections! _Can I count on you to do your part?_

clean_body: Peter, I'm not alone in believing you truly deserve this. My colleagues and I at House GOP Headquarters were all in agreement. In fact, we've made a unanimous decision. If there's anyone who has a record worthy of this, it's you. First of all, Congratulations! This is reserved only for top supporters of our movement. But I have to say, you've been so outstanding in your record of support that you've built up a profile that falls within the TOP Tier of GOP Patriots. one of House GOP's most trusted patriots, and you deserve to have the status to reflect that! And before you click, I want to let you know that we'll be leaning on you for your opinion on matters as we move forward. I'm putting this deal on the table because we can't afford to lose our most trusted allies when our majority is as thin as it is right now! You, God Bless You and God Bless America! Sincerely, Mike Johnson56th Speaker of the House On behalf of House GOP HQ (Platinum Committee)| | Paid for by the NRCC and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. name: Josh Orton
subject: Trump’s stacking the courts. We’re fighting back.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Demand Justice PAC
body:
[ https://act.demandjustice.org/go/54940?refcode=em20251203-60957&t=1001&akid=60957%2E1407376%2EkSVgLu ]Demand Justice PAC[ https://act.demandjustice.org/go/54940?refcode=em20251203-60957&t=1002&akid=60957%2E1407376%2EkSVgLu ]Demand Justice PAC

Since its inception, Demand Justice has used its bold voice in pursuit of
a federal judiciary that works for and represents everyday people — and
today, I’m excited to welcome you to this grassroots team.

We’re at a defining moment — not just for the courts, but for democracy
itself.

The threat we face is clear. In Donald Trump’s second term, he appears
determined not just to break the law, but to dismantle it — appointing
extremist judges and going after institutions, individuals, and anyone
else standing in his way.

Trump’s judges aren’t just loyalists — they are enabling a slide toward
oligarchy, siding with billionaires and corporations while stripping
workers, consumers, and communities of their rights. Meanwhile, Democratic
leaders have been too slow to push back.

At Demand Justice, we’re taking bold action:

* Holding officials accountable, including Senate Democrats, if they
shrink from this fight and vote to normalize authoritarianism with our
[ https://act.demandjustice.org/signup/202512-nocronyjudges-pac/?source=em20251203-60957&t=1003&akid=60957%2E1407376%2EkSVgLu ]No Crony Judges campaign.
* Exposing the economic stakes of Trump’s courts, showing how his
nominees tilt the system toward entrenched power.
* Mobilizing grassroots energy to hold lawmakers accountable and stop
extreme judicial nominees from taking lifetime seats.

This is where you come in. Demand Justice can only do this work with
people like you chipping in whatever you can afford to support our
efforts. Without you, we simply don’t exist.

Not enough people are acting to protect the courts from
Trump, so we’re grateful
you’re here. [ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dj-em-welcome?refcode=em20251203-60957&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=60957_1407376_kSVgLu&akid=60957%2E1407376%2EkSVgLu ]I’m respectfully asking if you will make a donation of
just $5 or more to Demand Justice PAC right now. We rely on grassroots
donations in our fight to restore integrity to the judiciary.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dj-em-welcome?amount=5&refcode=em20251203-60957&refcode2=60957_1407376_kSVgLu&akid=60957%2E1407376%2EkSVgLu ]Donate $5 ››

Whether or not today is the right day for you to donate, thank you for
joining Demand Justice. Every person matters in this critical, underserved
fight.

In solidarity,

Josh Orton
President

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dj-em-welcome?refcode=em20251203-60957&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&refcode2=60957_1407376_kSVgLu&akid=60957%2E1407376%2EkSVgLu ]Photo of Josh Orton, President of Demand Justice

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:

Receive Fewer Emails: https://act.demandjustice.org/survey/less-emails?t=1004&akid=60957%2E1407376%2EkSVgLu
Update Your Information: https://act.demandjustice.org/signup/update-your-information/?t=1005&akid=60957%2E1407376%2EkSVgLu
Unsubscribe: https://act.demandjustice.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?email=%%email%%&t=1006&akid=60957%2E1407376%2EkSVgLu
Email us: feedback@demandjustice.org

Paid for by Demand Justice PAC
611 Pennsylvania Ave SE, #192, Washington DC 20003
Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.
Contributions or gifts to Demand Justice PAC are not tax deductible.
